Provably fair

Two independent integrity guarantees: one for every spin outcome, one for every deposit receipt.

Slot spins

Every spin on a live Matrix Spins table is settled server-side from a seed we committed to before you placed your bet. After the spin, we reveal the full seed so you can recompute the reel stops in your browser with zero trust in our servers.

Before the spin, GET /api/slot/commit returns sha256(server_seed). You can snapshot that hash. After the spin, the response includes revealed.server_seed and a fresh next_commit.server_seed_hash — the used seed cannot be reused for a later round.

reel_float_i = parseInt(HMAC-SHA256(server_seed, client_seed + ":" + nonce + ":" + round).slice(i*8, i*8+8), 16) / 2^32
reel_index_i = floor(reel_float_i * reel_length)

The client_seed is yours to choose — use any ASCII string up to 64 characters; we substitute "default" if you leave it blank. The nonce increments within the life of a server seed and is reset to 0 each time a new commit is rolled, i.e. after every spin.

Verify a past spin →   (recomputes the reel stops in your browser via WebCrypto)  ·  See live RTP →

Deposit receipts

Every deposit that clears Stripe mints a receipt NFT to your account. The receipt's metadata is signed with HMAC-SHA256 using a server-held NFT_SIGNING_SECRET. A recorded signature cannot be altered without detection, and the token_id is deterministic — the same (user id, deposit id) pair always hashes to the same id.

What we sign

Every deposit that clears Stripe mints a receipt NFT to your account. The receipt's metadata is signed with HMAC-SHA256 using a server-held NFT_SIGNING_SECRET. A recorded signature cannot be altered without detection, and the token_id is deterministic — the same (user id, deposit id) pair always hashes to the same id.

token_id   = "msr_" + sha256("ms:" + user_id + ":" + deposit_id).slice(0, 24)
signature  = HMAC-SHA256(NFT_SIGNING_SECRET, JSON.stringify(metadata))

Metadata includes tier (bronze / silver / gold / platinum / diamond based on deposit amount), the amount + currency, the deposit id, the issue timestamp, and your user id. The image is an inline SVG rendered at mint time from the tier, so it's baked into the signature along with everything else.

Verifying a receipt

Your receipt JSON is available at GET /api/nfts/<token_id> while you're signed in. To verify independently:

  1. Take metadata and signature from the response.
  2. Ask our team for the signing-secret key that was active at the receipt's Issued timestamp. We publish the current key fingerprint so you can match the signature algorithm version without us revealing the secret on-chain.
  3. Compute HMAC-SHA256(secret, JSON.stringify(metadata)); compare to signature.

If the hex strings match, the receipt is authentic and unaltered since minting.

Scope notes

Receipts vs. spin outcomes: the receipt signature proves the receipt itself wasn't forged or altered. It does not prove the fairness of any specific spin — that proof comes from the commit-reveal chain described above (verify a round).

On-chain upgrade path

v1 receipts are DB-backed and off-chain. The receipt format is designed so the same token_id and metadata can later be published to an ERC-721 contract (we're evaluating Base and Polygon), at which point holders can verify ownership directly against the blockchain. Existing v1 receipts will remain valid and retain their token ids.

← Back to the app
